mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 3.0% signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates continuously when FEDFUNDS is in the easing regime.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 2.98% near easing threshold
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 5.0% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism activates when UNRATE crosses above the 5.0% threshold.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 4.8%, near trigger threshold
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 50% of instances when UNRATE > 5.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand that reduces corporate revenue growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism requires both series to decline simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline month-over-month
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 52% of instances when both RSAFS and HOUST decline, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 114.0 signals supply-side weakness and reduced capacity utilization, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice earnings expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 114.2334, near trigger threshold
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 50% of instances when INDPRO < 114.0, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint expansion in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals robust labor market momentum that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism requires both favorable labor signals simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises month-over-month AND UNRATE declines month-over-month
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with strong labor demand
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances when both PAYEMS rises and UNRATE declines, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises above 213.0 and the federal funds rate remains below 3.5%, the real interest rate becomes negative, signaling monetary accommodation that supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 54% of instances when real rates are negative, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in real GDP (GDPC1) above 11700 billion chained dollars signals strong economic expansion that supports corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism operates continuously in expansion regimes.
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 11677.1B, near expansion threshold
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 56% of instances when GDPC1 > 11700B, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE) signals stagflationary pressure that reduces equity risk appetite and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism requires both supply weakness and labor deterioration.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines month-over-month AND UNRATE rises month-over-month
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 51% of instances when both INDPRO declines and UNRATE rises, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).
